steps.title: Determine angle A
steps.content:
In right triangle ABC with the right angle at C and angle B = 30°, use the angle sum property:
$$A + 90 + 30 = 180$$
Therefore angle A = 60°.

steps.title: Apply 30-60-90 triangle side ratios
steps.content:
In a 30-60-90 triangle, the sides opposite the 30°, 60°, and 90° angles are in ratio $1: \sqrt{3}: 2$. With hypotenuse AB = 54:
  • AC (opposite the 30° angle) = $\frac{54}{2} = 27$
  • BC (opposite the 60° angle) = $\frac{54\sqrt{3}}{2} = 27\sqrt{3}$

steps.title: Calculate tan A and identify the answer
steps.content:
For angle A, tangent equals the opposite side divided by the adjacent side:
$$\tan A = \frac{BC}{AC} = \frac{27\sqrt{3}}{27} = \sqrt{3}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
